overrides: Fast Food Act compliance (Cal. Lab. Code §§ 1474-1475): franchisee solely responsible for determining applicability and compliance with health, safety, employment, minimum wage standards
Item 7 additional funds estimate accounts for Fast Food Act costs (e.g., increased wages)
No waiver of claims under state franchise law; no disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements
Registration notice: THESE FRANCHISES WILL BE OR HAVE BEEN FILED UNDER THE FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW
General release for renewal/transfer does NOT apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
Termination upon insolvency may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
Franchisee may bring actions in Maryland for claims under MD franchise law (unless preempted by federal law)
3-year statute of limitations for claims under MD franchise law
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
Franchisor must indemnify MN franchisees against third-party trademark infringement claims (per MN Dept of Commerce requirement)
90 days' notice of termination (60 days to cure) required per Minn. Stat. §80C.14
180 days' notice of non-renewal required
Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota per Minn. Stat. §80C.21
Cannot require waiver of jury trial
Cannot require consent to injunctive relief, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
General release for renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
General release does not apply to claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
Non-competition covenants generally considered unenforceable in ND (but franchisor will seek enforcement to extent possible)
Litigation venue: Atlanta GA, except franchisee may bring action in ND to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law
Governing law: North Dakota law applies to extent required by law
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
Assurance of Discontinuance with WA Attorney General: removed no-poach/employee solicitation restriction from form FA
Will not enforce no-poach provisions in existing agreements
Requested WA franchisees amend existing agreements to remove provision
No fines or monetary penalties assessed
